name: GraphRAG (Microsoft)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); LLM API costs significant for indexing (uses GPT-4 extensively)

auth.notes: OpenAI/Azure OpenAI API key required for indexing and querying

performance.notes: Global search queries require multiple LLM calls; expensive and slow; local search faster

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft's graph-based RAG approach. Indexes documents into knowledge graph with LLM-extracted entities, relationships, and community summaries. Two query modes: global (aggregated themes) and local (entity-specific). Dramatically better than naive RAG for 'what are the main themes?' type queries. High LLM costs for indexing — GPT-4 extensively used. Community detection algorithm for hierarchical summarization.

gotchas: Very high LLM API costs for indexing (easily $1-100+ per document corpus)
Indexing is slow (hours for large corpora)
Overkill for simple Q&A — use standard RAG instead
Requires careful prompt customization for domain-specific entity extraction
best_when: Thematic queries across large document corpora ('what are the main themes?')
Research synthesis across hundreds of papers or documents
Knowledge graph construction from unstructured documents
When naive RAG fails to answer holistic, summary-level questions
avoid_when: Simple Q&A with specific fact lookup (standard RAG faster and cheaper)
Budget-constrained projects (indexing costs can be very high)
summary: GraphRAG is Microsoft's graph-based RAG framework that builds knowledge graphs from documents using LLM-extracted entities and relationships. Enables holistic 'thematic' queries that naive RAG fails at. Two modes: global (theme discovery) and local (entity-specific). Very high LLM indexing costs. Best for research synthesis and theme discovery across large document corpora where standard RAG is insufficient.
